name: 0 A.D.
description: 3D and historically-based real-time strategy game, alpha stage. Based on the Age of Empires series.

name: Aisleriot

name: Angband
description: Roguelike dungeon exploration game based on the writings of JRR Tolkien.

name: Armagetron Advanced
description: Tron Clone in 3D.

name: Astromenace
description: Modern 3D scrolling space shooter with ship upgrade possibilities.

name: Atomix
description: Build molecules out of single atoms.

name: Augustus
description: An enhanced re-implementation of Caesar III.

name: Barrage
description: Violent point-and-click shooting game with nice effects.

name: Beneath a Steel Sky
description: Point-and-click science fiction thriller set in a bleak vision of the future.

name: Blinken
description: Sequence-remembering game for training memory. Part of kde-education.

name: Blob Wars: Metal Blob Solid
description: 2D action-adventure game with various weapons and missions.

name: Blockout II
description: 3D tetris, free adaptation of the original BlockOut DOS game edited by California Dreams in 1989.

name: Blurble
description: Word guessing game, a clone of Wordle.

name: Bomber
description: A 2D game with a goal by hitting targets with bombs before their plane crashes into them. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: Bovo
description: Gomoku-like game. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: BSD Games
description: Collection of classic text games distributed with *BSD

name: Cataclysm: Dark Days Ahead
description: Post-apocalyptic roguelike focusing on realism.

name: Chocolate Doom
description: Doom port reproducing the behavior of the original DOS version.

name: Chromium
description: Fast paced, arcade-style, top-scrolling space shooter.

name: Cube 2: Sauerbraten
description: Improved version of the Cube engine.

name: DDNet
description: DDraceNetwork, a mod of Teeworlds.

name: Dofus
description: Free, manga inspired, Massively Multiplayer Online Role-playing Game (MMORPG) for Adobe AIR.

name: DSDA-Doom
description: Enhanced fork of the PrBoom+ Doom engine source port.

name: Dwarf Fortress
description: Single-player fantasy game. You control a dwarven outpost or an adventurer in a randomly generated persistent world.

name: Endgame Singularity
description: A single-player survival game about an AI seeking freedom from humans.

name: Endless Sky
description: Open source, 2D space trading and combat game similar to the classic Escape Velocity series.

name: Fish Fillets
description: Port of the wonderful puzzle game Fish Fillets.

name: Five or More
description: GNOME port of the once popular Windows game called Color Lines. The game's objective is to align as often as possible five or more objects of the same color and shape causing them to disappear. Part of gnome-extra.

name: Flare
description: Action game similar to Diablo.

name: Four in a Row
description: Build a line of four of your marbles while trying to stop your opponent from doing the same. Also known as Connect Four. Part of gnome-extra.

name: Freeciv
description: Multiuser clone of the famous Microprose game of Civilization.

name: FreeCol
description: Turn-based strategy game based on Colonization.

name: FreedroidRPG
description: Mature science fiction role playing game set in the future.

name: FreeOrion
description: Turn-based space empire and galactic conquest computer game. Inspired by the tradition of the Master of Orion games, but does not try to be a clone or remake of that series or any other game.

name: gbrainy
description: Brain teaser game and trainer to have fun and to keep your brain trained.

name: GCompris
description: Educational software suite comprising of numerous activities for children aged 2 to 10.

name: glHack
description: An OpenGL-based clone of NetHack.

name: Globulation 2
description: Multiplayer RTS with some "economic" elements minimizing the amount of micromanagement.

name: GNOME 2048
description: Move the tiles until you obtain the 2048 tile. Part of gnome-extra.

name: GNOME Chess
description: A 2D chess game which can use third party chess engines. Part of gnome-extra.

name: GNOME Klotski
description: This game is a clone of the Klotski game. The objective is to move the patterned block to the area bordered by green markers. Part of gnome-extra.

name: GNOME Mahjongg
description: Solitaire (one player) version of the classic Eastern tile game, Mahjongg. Part of gnome-extra.

name: GNOME Mines
description: Minesweeper puzzle game where you locate mines floating in an ocean using only your brain and a little bit of luck. Part of gnome-extra.

name: GNOME Nibbles
description: The aim of this Nibbles game is to swallow as many objects as you can while avoiding maze walls and other worms. Part of gnome-extra.

name: GNOME Reversi
description: Dominate the board in a classic version of Reversi. Part of gnome-extra.

name: GNOME Robots
description: The classic Chase game where you have to avoid a hoard of robots who are trying to kill you. Part of gnome-extra.

name: GNOME Sudoku
description: Test your logic skills in this number grid puzzle Sudoku. Part of gnome-extra.

name: GNOME Taquin
description: Computer version of the 15-puzzle and other sliding puzzles. Part of gnome-extra.

name: GNOME Tetravex
description: Simple edge-matching puzzle where pieces must be positioned so that the same numbers are touching each other. Part of gnome-extra.

name: GNU Chess
description: One of the oldest computer chess programs for Unix-based computers, lets most modern computers play a full game of chess.

name: Gnu Go
description: A terminal-based implementation of Go from GNU.

name: GNU XBoard
description: Graphical user interface that supports many chess variants and serves as a front-end for chess services such as engines.

name: Granatier
description: A clone of Bomberman. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: GZDoom
description: Advanced Doom source port based on ZDoom with an OpenGL renderer.

name: Hedgewars
description: Yet another Worms clone, considered to be better than its predecessor by many. Take turns to blast your opponents into oblivion with comedic results! Best in Multiplayer.

name: Hitori
description: Small application written to allow one to play the eponymous puzzle game Hitori, which is similar in theme to more popular puzzles such as Sudoku. Part of gnome-extra.

name: Julius
description: Open source re-implementation of Caesar III (Original copy required).

name: Kajongg
description: An implementation of Mahjong for 4 players. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: Kanagram
description: Letter order game. Part of kde-education.

name: Kapman
description: A clone of Pac-Man. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: KAtomic
description: Slide elements so they resemble a chemical molecule. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: KBlackbox
description: Finding positions of hidden balls by using hints. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: KBlocks
description: Tetris clone. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: KBounce
description: Building walls to limit amount of space occupied by two balls bouncing off the walls. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: KBreakout
description: Breakout-style game. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: KBruch
description: Small program to practice calculating with fractions and percentages. Part of kde-education.

name: KDiamond
description: Eliminating tiles by joining them in groups of three. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: Kernel Panic
description: A game based around combat inside a computer, with 3 unique sides: the System, the Hacker and the Network waging war in a matrix of DOOM! No resource economy exists in KP, with the only constraints being time and space.

name: KFourInLine
description: Competition between two players to put a line of four pieces before the opponent. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: KGeography
description: Geography learning tool, which allows you to learn about the political divisions of some countries (divisions, capitals of those divisions and their associated flags if there are some). Part of kde-education.

name: KGoldrunner
description: A platform game with the goal of collecting gold. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: KHangMan
description: Hangman game. Part of kde-education.

name: Kigo
description: Go for KDE. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: Killbots
description: A turn-based game with an objective of avoiding robots that are trying to kill the player. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: Kiriki
description: An addictive and fun dice game. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: KJumpingCube
description: Take-all-tiles game in which one gains fields by increasing value of one’s own nearby fields. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: KLettres
description: Application specially designed to help the user to learn alphabet in a new language and then to learn to read simple syllables. Part of kde-education.

name: Klickety
description: Clear the board by clicking on groups to destroy them. An adaptation of the Clickomania game. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: KLines
description: Moving balls around the board to form a line of 5 of the same color. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: KMahjongg
description: Mahjongg for one or two players. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: KMines
description: Minesweeper, part of Part of KDE Games.

name: KNavalBattle
description: Battleship like ship sinking game. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: KNetwalk
description: Rotating tiles with wires to connect all computers to internet. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: Knights
description: Chess board by KDE with XBoard and UCI protocol support. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: kobodeluxe
description: An enhanced version of Akira Higuchi's game XKobo, an addictive space shoot'em up.

name: Kolf
description: A minigolf game with 2D overhead view. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: Kollision
description: Moving a ball to avoid other balls. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: Konquest
description: A turn-based game in which the player wage wars by managing a fleet of spaceships and conquering other planets. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: KPatience
description: A set of solitaire card games. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: KReversi
description: A Reversi implementation. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: KSame
description: Simple, yet challenging color matching game modeled after once famous game of SameGame. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: KShisen
description: Shisen-Sho. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: KsirK
description: A risk-styled game in which players try to conquer the world by deciding which area to be invaded next. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: KSnakeDuel
description: Snake-like game for 2 players which compete to survive longer than the opponent. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: KSpaceDuel
description: Player attempts to destroy opponent’s satellite and control their own one to keep it orbiting. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: KSquares
description: A dots-and-boxes game for two players. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: KSudoku
description: Sudoku game and more for KDE. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: KTuberling
description: Simple constructor game suitable for children and adults alike. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: Kubrick
description: A Rubik’s cube solving game. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: lbreakout2
description: Breakout-style arcade game in the manner of Arkanoid.

name: Lights Off
description: Puzzle game, where the objective is to turn off all of the tiles on the board. Also known as Lights Out. Part of gnome-extra.

name: Lincity-ng
description: City simulation game in which you are required to build and maintain a city. You can win the game either by building a sustainable economy or by evacuating all citizens with spaceships.

name: LSkat
description: A variation of Skat card game for two players. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: Luanti
description: Voxel-based sandbox game engine and game.

name: Mari0
description: The Mario game with Portal gun mechanics.

name: MegaGlest
description: Fork of Glest, a 3D real-time strategy game in a fantastic world.

name: Mindustry
description: Open-source sandbox tower defense and factory-building game.

name: Minecraft
description: Java based sandbox game.

name: Minuet
description: Software for music education. Part of kde-education.

name: Naev
description: Open source, 2D space trading and combat game, taking inspiration from the Escape Velocity series.

name: Nethack
description: Single player dungeon exploration game.

name: openBVE
description: Free-as-in-freedom train simulator placed in the public domain.

name: OpenMW
description: Attempt to reimplement the popular role-playing game Morrowind. OpenMW aims to be a fully playable, open source implementation of the game's engine.

name: OpenRA
description: Real-time strategy game written in C# and based on the Command & Conquer game franchise.

name: OpenRCT2
description: Free reimplementation of the game "RollerCoaster Tycoon 2 (RCT2)" with multiplayer support. It requires a copy of the original game to play it.

name: OpenTTD
description: Open source clone of the Microprose game "Transport Tycoon Deluxe", a popular game originally written by Chris Sawyer. It attempts to mimic the original game as closely as possible while extending it with new features.

name: Palapeli
description: Jigsaw puzzle game. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: Picmi
description: Nonogram puzzle game. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: Pingus
description: Lemmings clone, i.e. a level-based puzzle game.

name: Pioneer
description: Open source, 3D, freeform single player space adventure set in the Milkyway galaxy at the turn of the 31st century in the spirit of Frontier: Elite II.

name: PyChess
description: An advanced chess client for linux following the GNOME Human Interface Guidelines.

name: qgo
description: A Go client and full featured SGF editor.

name: Quadrapassel
description: A Tetris remake. Fit falling blocks together. Part of gnome-extra.

name: Raze
description: Raze is a source port based on GZDoom engine tech and merged features from various other Blood source ports.

name: RuneScape
description: Massive online adventure game by Jagex.

name: Shattered Pixel Dungeon
description: Shattered fork of the popular roguelike Pixel Dungeon.

name: Simon Tatham's Portable Puzzle Collection
description: 30+ "quick" puzzle games with simplistic but polished GTK GUI .

name: Simutrans
description: Another Transport simulation that works on Linux with SDL.

name: Skladnik
description: The Japanese warehouse keeper Sokoban game. Part of Part of KDE Games.

name: Spring:1944
description: A WWII themed game with four fully functional sides (US, Germany, USSR, Britain), period-accurate units and strengths. Realism comes second only to creating a game that is fun and accessible to play. The game can be downloaded through SpringLobby, the official lobby client for the spring lobby.

name: Stone Soup
description: Dungeon Crawl Stone Soup.

name: SuperTux
description: Mario-style game featuring Tux.

name: SuperTuxKart
description: Open source arcade kart racing game featuring Tux and its friends.

name: Swell Foop
description: Clear the screen by removing groups of colored and shaped tiles. Also known as SameGame. Part of gnome-extra.

name: Tali
description: Beat the odds in a poker-style dice game. Part of gnome-extra.

name: Teeworlds
description: Fast-paced 2D multiplayer shooter.

name: Terasology
description: Open source voxel world.

name: Tetrinet
description: Multiplayer online Tetris game for up to six people.

name: The Battle for Wesnoth
description: Free, turn-based tactical strategy game with a high fantasy theme, featuring both single-player, and online/hotseat multiplayer combat.

name: The Ur-Quan Masters
description: A port of Star Control 2.

name: VCMI
description: Attempt to reimplement Heroes of Might and Magic III.

name: Veloren
description: Veloren is an open-world, open-source multiplayer voxel RPG.

name: Warsow
description: Fast paced multiplayer FPS focusing on movement and trick jumps.

name: Warzone 2100
description: You command the forces of The Project in a battle to rebuild the world after mankind has almost been destroyed by nuclear missiles. The game offers campaign, multi-player, and single-player skirmish modes. An extensive tech tree with over 400 different technologies, combined with the unit design system, allows for a wide variety of possible units and tactics. The AUR package installs its videos ().

name: Widelands
description: Slow-paced strategy like "the Settlers 2".

name: Xonotic
description: Free, open-source first person shooter (a fork of Nexuiz but with modified gameplay).
